Home / Workflows / Automated Schedule Production
Live 3 phases · 10 steps

Automated Schedule Production

Generates MEP schedules from the live Revit model — equipment, duct, pipe and cable tray schedules in one prompt, validated against project standards.

Try this workflow → All workflows
Schedule production walkthrough

Prerequisites

  • Revit model with placed MEP families
  • Project standards template loaded
  • Schedule Builder app installed

Outputs

  • Equipment schedules (PDF + Excel)
  • Duct and pipe sizing schedules
  • Cable tray schedules
  • Validation report against standards

Workflow phases

Model audit Schedule generation Validation and export
Phase 1 of 3

Model audit

Scan the Revit model for placed families, parameter completeness, and naming compliance.

1
Snapshot current model state Read all MEP elements via get_elements_in_view and build a working inventory.
2
Check parameter coverage Flag any families missing required schedule parameters (size, load, circuit, etc).
3
Validate naming conventions Cross-check family and type names against the project naming standard.
Tools used
  • get_elements_in_view
  • snapshot_query
  • get_project_standards
Phase 2 of 3

Schedule generation

Build each schedule type from the validated element set, applying project formatting.

1
Generate equipment schedule Group by system, sort by mark, populate all required columns.
2
Generate duct schedule Extract duct runs, calculate total lengths, group by system abbreviation.
3
Generate pipe schedule Extract pipe segments, pressures, insulation thickness by system type.
4
Generate cable tray schedule List tray runs with fill ratio and circuit reference.
Tools used
  • create_schedule
  • set_parameter_value
  • list_rooms
Phase 3 of 3

Validation and export

Run QA checks on the generated schedules and export to PDF and Excel.

1
Cross-check totals Compare schedule totals against model element counts to catch omissions.
2
Apply formatting template Set column widths, header styles and page breaks per the project standard.
3
Export outputs Render PDF sheets and Excel workbooks to the project output folder.
Tools used
  • export_schedule
  • qa_check
  • get_document_info

No related workflows yet.
